recalls.Description: TopHomer has issued a recall for its 8-inch pool drain covers due to significant safety concerns regarding entrapment and drowning. These white ABS plastic covers have a top grate measuring 7.2 inches in diameter and a bottom mounting plate of 8 inches. Key markings include 'Listed suction fitting,' 'SP-1053-B,' and '88D9 148 GPM' on the grate's center, along with a warning to secure the grate with screws on the outer ring.
recalls.Title: TopHomer Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Serious Entrapment and Drowning Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should contact TopHomerUS via email at Tophomer.recall@outlook.com or visit the TopHomerUS Amazon Seller Profile page for further assistance.

Images.Caption: Recalled TopHomer Pool Drain Cover
Recalled TopHomer Pool Drain Cover and Base

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon.com from August 2024 to October 2025, priced between $15 and $26.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment and drowning hazards due to non-compliance with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, they should remove the cover, photograph it, and email the image to Tophomer.recall@outlook.com. It's essential to ensure all pools have VGBA-compliant drain covers and educate children to avoid drains.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: TopHomer pool drain covers pose serious safety risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure pool safety.

recalls.Description: Jeriflyer is recalling its 8-inch pool drain covers due to a serious risk of entrapment, which can lead to severe injury or death. These white plastic drain covers, measuring 8.1 inches in diameter, do not meet the safety requirements outlined in the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act. This poses a significant danger to swimmers and bathers using pools equipped with these covers.
recalls.Title: Jeriflyer Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Entrapment Hazard; Non-compliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to Jeriflyer via email at Junjuanshop2025@outlook.com or visit Amazon.com: Jerliflyer and click on 'Recall' at the top of the page for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover
Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover
Recalled Jeriflyer pool drain cover

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon from April 2024 to May 2025, priced between $10 and $15.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with safety standards

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, remove the drain cover, take a photo of it, and email it to Junjuanshop2025@outlook.com. Ensure all pools have VGBA compliant covers and educate children about drain safety. Junjuanshop and Amazon will contact all known buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Jeriflyer recalls 8-inch pool drain covers due to entrapment risks. Immediate action required for safety and refunds.

recalls.Description: Endless Pools is recalling its Manual Retractable Security Pool Covers due to serious safety concerns. These covers, which feature a hand crank mechanism for easy operation, were available in various colors such as tan, black, gray, light blue, and royal blue, and were customized to fit pools ranging from 8 to 16 feet wide. Improper installation can create a dangerous gap of over 4.5 inches between the cover roller and the pool surface, failing to meet safety standards and posing drowning and entrapment hazards to children.
recalls.Title: Endless Pools Recalls Manual Retractable Pool Covers Due to Drowning and Entrapment Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to Endless Pools at 800-910-2714 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at poolhelp@endlesspools.com, or visit https://myendlesspool.com/s/support/product-recalls for more information.

Products.Name: Manual Retractable Security Pool Covers
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 11,500 (In addition, about 480 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Manual Retractable Pool Security Cover in open position
Wall Mount Configuration
Below Deck Mount Configuration

Retailers.Name: Available through Endless Pools and authorized dealers nationwide, as well as online at www.endlesspools.com from January 1994 through December 2024, priced between $1,000 and $2,200.

Hazards.Name: Drowning and entrapment hazards due to improper installation

Remedies.Name: Free inspection and repair by Endless Pools support services

recalls.metaDescription: Endless Pools recalls Manual Retractable Pool Covers due to drowning and entrapment risks. Contact for free inspection and repair.

recalls.Description: The NICREW Submersible RGB LED Lights, model number N21743, are being recalled due to a serious risk of injury or death from ingestion. These lights, sold in a 15-inch black tube, come with a remote control featuring 21 buttons and a CR2032 lithium coin battery, which poses a significant hazard to children. The product fails to meet federal safety regulations regarding button cell batteries, lacking necessary warnings about the dangers of ingestion.
recalls.Title: NICREW Submersible RGB LED Lights Recalled Due to Ingestion Hazard and Federal Regulation Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact Nicrew at nicrew@support.com or visit www.nicrew.com/n21743-recall/. You can also find details by clicking on Product Recalls at the bottom of the Nicrew homepage.

Products.Name: NICREW 15" Submersible RGB LED Lights with Remote Control

Images.Caption: Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control
Recalled Submersible RGB LED Light with Remote Control packaging

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.Amazon.com from May 2024 to August 2024 for approximately $17.

Distributors.Name: Nicrew Technology Co. Limited, of China

Hazards.Name: The remote control's lithium coin battery can be accessed by children, creating an ingestion risk that can lead to severe injuries or death.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the product immediately, remove the battery, and contact Nicrew for a free replacement remote. To receive the replacement, email your Amazon order number and a photo of the destroyed remote to nicrew@support.com. Follow local hazardous waste procedures for battery disposal.

recalls.metaDescription: NICREW recalls Submersible RGB LED Lights due to ingestion hazards. Contact Nicrew for replacements and safety information.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for the Aiper Seagull Pro cordless robotic pool vacuum cleaner, model ZT6001. This device weighs around 24 pounds and has dimensions of approximately 19.5 x 18.5 x 15 inches. The brand name "Aiper" is displayed prominently on the front of the unit, while the model name and serial number, starting with S/N "61", can be found on the label located on the bottom of the vacuum.
recalls.Title: Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Vacuum Cleaners Recalled for Fire and Burn Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the affected pool vacuums immediately and reach out to Aiper at 888-968-0666 from 9 a.m. to 9 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@aiper.com or visit https://aiper.com/seagullpro-recall/ for further details.

Products.Name: Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Vacuum Cleaner
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 32,660 (In addition, about 2,530 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Aiper Seagull Pro Cordless Robotic Pool Cleaner
Label with model name and serial number located on the bottom of the recalled pool vacuum

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the recalled pool vacuums melting, smoking or catching on fire while charging causing five incidents of property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Manufacturers.Name: Dongguan Yuanhong Intelligent Technology Co. Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: Available at Best Buy, Home Depot, Lowe's, and Walmart stores nationwide, as well as online at www.aiper.com, Amazon.com, Bestbuy.com, HomeDepot.com, Lowes.com, and Walmart.com from March 2023 through May 2024, priced between $700 and $900.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Aiper Intelligent Co. Ltd., of China

Hazards.Name: Overheating current adapter leading to burn and fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Consumers will receive a complimentary replacement Aiper Scuba S1 robotic pool cleaner after returning the recalled unit. Aiper will provide a free return label and ship the replacement within 15 days of receiving the Seagull Pro.

recalls.metaDescription: Aiper recalls Seagull Pro cordless robotic pool cleaners due to fire risks. Contact Aiper for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: GOETAS has issued a recall for its pool drain covers due to a serious entrapment hazard. These white, plastic drain covers, designed for swimming pools, are approximately 7 inches high with top grates measuring around 9 inches in diameter. The products do not meet the safety requirements set by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act, posing risks to swimmers and bathers.
recalls.Title: GOETAS Pool Drain Covers Recalled for Entrapment Risk; Non-Compliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further details, consumers can reach out to GOETAS via email at Atlaspool@163.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – with top grate
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – without top grate
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – side view
Recalled GOETAS Pool Drain Cover – front view

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively on Amazon.com from March 2022 through July 2024 for approximately $61.

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with safety regulations.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled drain covers immediately, remove and dispose of them, and take a photo of the cover to send to GOETAS for a full refund. It's crucial to ensure all pool drains comply with safety standards and educate children about the dangers of drains. Amazon will contact all known buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: GOETAS pool drain covers pose an entrapment hazard. Immediate action required for safety compliance.

recalls.Description: TonGass is recalling its 8-inch round pool drain covers, specifically Model WGX1048E, which were sold for swimming pool use. These plastic drain covers, featuring two screws and measuring 7-7/8 inches in diameter, do not meet the safety standards set by the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act, creating a potential entrapment hazard for swimmers and bathers.
recalls.Title: Recall of TonGass Pool Drain Covers Due to Safety Compliance Issues and Entrapment Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach out to TonGass via email at tongass@126.com, visit their Amazon Seller Profile, or call Amazon's toll-free customer service at 888-280-4331.

Images.Caption: Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (top view)
Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (bottom view)
Recalled TonGass pool drain cover (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from September 2023 to June 2024 for $23.

Importers.Name: Oneida International Ltd., dba TonGass, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with VGBA safety standards

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled drain covers immediately. To obtain a refund, take a photo of the removed drain cover and email it to tongass@126.com. Ensure that all pools and spas are equipped with VGBA compliant drain covers, and educate children about the dangers of drains. TonGass will contact all purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: TonGass recalls pool drain covers due to safety violations, posing entrapment risks. Learn how to get a refund and ensure pool safety.

recalls.Description: TOPINCN is recalling its 1.5-inch and 3-inch round pool drain covers, which are used in swimming pools. The 1.5-inch drain cover has an outer diameter of approximately 4 inches and an inner diameter of about 2 inches, while the 3-inch version measures around 5 inches in outer diameter and 3 inches in inner diameter. Both covers are white and constructed from plastic, featuring two screws for installation.
recalls.Title: Recall of TOPINCN Pool Drain Covers Due to Entrapment Risk; Non-Compliance with Safety Standards
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, contact Sanure via email at sanure710@126.com, or visit their Amazon storefront at https://www.amazon.com/sp?ie=UTF8&isAmazonFulfilled=1&marketplaceSeller=1&orderID=114-2704361-2776233&seller=A3FYRQNET3AI4U.

Images.Caption: Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (top view)
Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (bottom view)
Recalled TOPINCN pool drain cover (side view)

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at www.amazon.com from September 2022 to March 2024, priced between $9 and $32.

Importers.Name: Dong Guan Shi Li Peng Zhi Dian Zi Shang Wu You Xian Gong Si, dba Sanure Storefront, of China

Hazards.Name: Entrapment hazard due to non-compliance with the Virginia Graeme Baker Pool and Spa Safety Act (VGBA).

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled drain covers immediately, remove them from pools, take a photo, and email it to sanure710@126.com for a full refund. Ensure all pool and spa drain covers meet VGBA standards and educate children to avoid drains. Sanure will reach out to all buyers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: TOPINCN recalls pool drain covers due to entrapment risks. Immediate action required for safety compliance.
